coverage_note: Regional metocean / environmental design criteria for support-vessel operability limits (AHTS, PSV, CTV, SOV/W2W). Records are structured per region. STRONGEST DATA (full extreme-value tables with explicit return-period columns, extracted from public PDFs via pdftotext): (1) North Sea / Buchan Deep from the publicly published Hywind Buchan Deep Metocean Design Basis (marine.gov.scot), and (2) Gulf of Mexico West and West-Central regions from API Bulletin 2INT-MET (free copy on law.resource.org). These give omni-directional Hs, Hmax, Tp, 1-hr/10-min/3-s wind at 10 m, and surface/profile current by return period - directly usable for operability/persistence limits. WEAKER DATA (single headline numbers from search-result summaries of paywalled OnePetro/ResearchGate papers, NOT verified in primary tables): West Africa (Forcados Hs100=3.02 m), Brazil/Campos (Hs50=8.77 m, Hs100=9.54 m), NW Australia/North-West Shelf (cyclone-dominated, qualitative only - no numeric table obtained). The current authoritative GoM standard is API RP 2MET (2nd ed., paywalled); 2INT-MET (2007) is its free interim predecessor and values differ slightly from the latest RP. DNV-RP-C205 itself is paywalled (DNV store); it is captured as a METHODOLOGY record (scatter diagrams, joint Hs-Tp distributions, wind/current profiles) rather than region-specific numbers. Operability LIMITS for the vessels themselves (e.g. CTV access Hs~1.5 m, SOV/W2W gangway Hs~2.5-3.5 m, AHTS/PSV working Hs) are vendor/contract-specific and were NOT found as a single citable public table - flagged as gap. Vessel-class fields (year_built, etc.) are N/A for this environmental-criteria layer. Access date for all web sources: 2026-06-18.

verdict.summary: Verified against primary sources by downloading both cited PDFs and extracting tables with pdftotext (exactly as the dataset claims it was produced). This is an environmental-design-criteria dataset, not a vessel dataset: there are no hull dimensions/displacement/SWL/RAO to vet, and those fields are correctly N/A. The two STRONG records are immaculate transcriptions. North Sea / Buchan Deep: omni Hs 7.8/9.4/10.9/13.8 m (1/10/50/100yr) matches Table 3.8 exactly; associated Tp mean/P5/P95 at Hs50 and Hs100 correct; 1-hr 10m wind omni 26.5/29.5/31.0 (1/10/50yr) matches Table 2.11 exactly. GoM West region (Table 4.5.1-1A) and West-Central region (Table 4.5.2-1A): every value across all 8 return periods (Hs, Hmax, max crest, Tp, 1-hr wind, 3s gust, surface current) matches the 2INT-MET tables exactly; region longitude bounds (97.5-95.0W; 94.0-90.5W) correct. The WEAK records are honestly self-flagged as estimated/secondary and were independently corroborated by web search: Campos Basin Hs50=8.77/Hs100=9.54 m and West Africa Forcados Hs100=3.02 m both confirmed against the cited papers. Cited URLs are real and on-topic. No hard number is dressed as fact without a citation or estimated/gap flag. All values physically plausible for the respective regimes (North Sea storm waves, GoM hurricane extremes, West Africa swell, Brazil extra-tropical swell). No fabrication detected. Two minor transcription/labeling issues found, neither rising to fabrication (both are hedged or source-faithful).

flagged_records.name: North Sea (Buchan Deep) - omni-directional extreme design criteria
flagged_records.issue: surface_current_omni_1yr_cms is given as '139 (0deg sector)' and surface_current_omni_50yr_cms is hedged as 'estimated ~136-139, full omni row not isolated - see gap'. The actual Table 4.9 omni (0-360) row IS clearly present and isolated: 155/170/180/184 cm/s for 1/10/50/100yr. The 139 figure is actually the 0deg sector 100yr value, mislabeled as 1yr omni. The true 1yr omni surface current is 155 cm/s. Honestly gap-flagged so not fabrication, but the stated value is wrong and the 'not isolated' claim is incorrect - the omni row exists in the extracted text.

flagged_records.name: North Sea (Buchan Deep) - omni-directional extreme design criteria
flagged_records.issue: Non-physical kink in the EVA curve: Hs jumps +1.5 m from 50yr (10.9) to 100yr (13.8), far larger than the +1.5 m over the 10->50yr span. VERIFIED this kink is present verbatim in source Table 3.8, so it is a source characteristic (small-sample/contour-method artifact), NOT a dataset error. Flagged only so downstream users do not treat the 100yr Hs as a smooth extrapolation.

flagged_records.name: Gulf of Mexico - West-Central region
flagged_records.issue: Record is titled 'West-Central' and cites the source caption as 'West Gulf of Mexico (94.0W to 90.5W)'. The source PDF itself uses inconsistent captions for this table (4.5.2-1A reads 'West Gulf' while the TOC/4.5.2-1B read 'West Central / Western Central'). Longitude bounds and all numeric values are correct; this is a source-caption quirk, not a dataset error.

flagged_records.name: West Africa (Gulf of Guinea) - swell-dominated regime
flagged_records.issue: The Hs100=3.02 m figure is attributed to 'WASP/WAX summaries' with a WASP URL, but the 3.02 m / mean-period-7s value actually originates from a separate Forcados/Asabo offshore-Nigeria sea-state paper, not the WASP report. Number is correct and corroborated, URL is real and on-topic for the regime, and the record is explicitly marked estimated/secondary/gap - so loose attribution, not fabrication.

records.name: North Sea (Buchan Deep, offshore E Scotland) - omni-directional extreme design criteria

fields.data_basis: NORA10 hindcast (waves/wind) 1958-2009 + on-site current measurements; NORSOK N-003 extremes
fields.surface_current_omni_50yr_cms: estimated:read 0-deg dominant sector ~136-139 cm/s; full omni row not isolated in extraction - see gap
fields.directional_dist_provided: yes - 12 x 30deg sectors for wind, wave, current (Tables 2.11, 3.9, 4.9)
fields.monthly_dist_provided: yes - seasonal/monthly tables for operability windows (Tables 2.12, 3.10)
fields.directional_to_omni_uplift: +~10% on directional extremes at 10^-2 annual prob per NORSOK procedure
fields.operability_relevance: Seasonal/monthly + directional scatter supports W2W/SOV gangway and CTV access persistence analysis

gaps: exact omni-directional surface current 50yr/100yr value (only dominant 0deg sector read)
Hs-Tp scatter diagram cell counts (figure-only in PDF)
vessel-specific operability Hs thresholds

citations.source: Hywind Buchan Deep Metocean Design Basis RE2014-002 Rev 2, Table 3.8 (omni-directional extreme Hs and associated Tp)
Hywind Buchan Deep Metocean Design Basis RE2014-002 Rev 2, Table 2.11/2.12 (1-hr mean wind extremes, omni row 0deg-360deg = 26.5/29.5/31.0)
Hywind Buchan Deep Metocean Design Basis RE2014-002 Rev 2, Sec 1.1 and Table 4.9 (current speed extremes)

records.name: Gulf of Mexico - West region (97.5W-95.0W), hurricane-driven extremes

fields.region: US Gulf of Mexico - Western (97.5W to 95.0W)
fields.applicability: Waves listed for WD >= 1000 m; currents WD >= 150 m; wind all depths; north of 26N
fields.data_basis: API 2INT-MET interim hurricane criteria (synthetic storm / hindcast)
fields.operability_relevance: Hurricane-season extremes; operability windows for PSV/AHTS dominated by tropical-cyclone avoidance, not these design extremes

gaps: non-hurricane (winter-storm / Loop Current) operational sea-state scatter
shallow-water (<1000m) Hs via depth-decay curves are figures only
latest API RP 2MET 2nd-ed values (paywalled, differ from this interim doc)

citations.source: API Bulletin 2INT-MET, Table 4.5.1-1A Independent Extreme Values for Hurricane Winds, Waves, Currents and Surge, Western Gulf of Mexico

records.name: Gulf of Mexico - West-Central region (94.0W-90.5W), hurricane-driven extremes

fields.region: US Gulf of Mexico - West-Central (94.0W to 90.5W)
fields.applicability: Waves WD >= 1000 m; currents WD >= 150 m; wind all depths; north of 26N

gaps: Central (89.5W-86.5W) and Eastern (85.5W-82.5W) GoM regional tables not transcribed here (present in same source Tables 4.5.3 / 4.5.4)
Loop Current operational currents
latest API RP 2MET values

citations.source: API Bulletin 2INT-MET, Table 4.5.2-1A Independent Extreme Values for Hurricane Winds, Waves, Currents and Surge, West Gulf of Mexico (94.0W to 90.5W)

records.name: West Africa (Gulf of Guinea - Nigeria/Angola), swell-dominated regime

fields.region: West Africa - Gulf of Guinea (Nigeria, Cabinda, Angola)
fields.regime: Persistent low-Hs long-period swell from S/SW (South Atlantic 40-60S storms) + secondary N Atlantic swell; benign winds, weak tropical-cyclone influence
fields.Hs_100yr_m_Forcados: 3.02 (estimated/secondary - Forcados offshore S Nigeria; from WASP study summary, primary table not verified)
fields.mean_wave_period_s: approx 7 (associated with Forcados Hs100); swell Tp commonly 12-16 s
fields.operability_relevance: Low Hs but long-period swell drives vessel/gangway roll & heave resonance - operability often period-limited not height-limited; favourable for high CTV/W2W uptime if motion-tuned
fields.design_standard_context: Project metocean studies aligned to ISO 19901-1 / API RP 2MET regional approach

gaps: primary-source extreme-value table (Hs/Tp/current by return period) - values here are search-summary headline numbers from paywalled OnePetro/ResearchGate (WASP/WAX); NOT verified in original tables
current profile design values
surface current extremes
directional swell scatter diagram

citations.source: West Africa Swell Project (WASP) and West Africa hindcast (WAX) summaries; Metocean Design Criteria for Deep Water Offshore Systems (ResearchGate) - secondary, headline values only

records.name: Brazil (Campos/Santos Basin, offshore Rio de Janeiro), extra-tropical swell regime

fields.region: SE Brazil - Campos / northern Santos Basin
fields.regime: Energetic SW swell from extra-tropical cyclones/anticyclones (winter/autumn dominant) + cold-front events; strong Brazil Current
fields.current_regime: Brazil Current + meso-scale eddies; strong surface/sub-surface currents (design values project-specific)
fields.operability_relevance: SW swell + strong current affects DP station-keeping (AHTS/PSV) and W2W headings; current is a primary operability driver alongside Hs

gaps: primary extreme-value tables (Hs/Tp/wind/current by return period) - values are search-summary numbers from paywalled OnePetro/ResearchGate papers, NOT verified in original tables
Brazil Current design speed-vs-depth profile
Hs-Tp scatter diagram
wind extremes

citations.source: Extreme Wave Analysis in Campos Basin (extra-tropical cyclones/anticyclones); Metocean Measurements at Northern Santos Basin (OTC-20947-MS) - secondary, headline values only

records.name: NW Australia (North-West Shelf), tropical-cyclone-dominated regime

fields.regime: Extreme sea states dominated by tropical cyclones; ~4.5 storms/season, avg ~2 reach Category 5; benign non-cyclone background
fields.Hs_100yr_m: gap (cyclone-driven; site-specific synthetic-storm databases used - no single public value verified)
fields.design_methodology: Synthetic tropical-cyclone databases to 100/1000/10000-yr levels; joint extreme current-with-depth profiles
fields.operability_relevance: Long benign windows punctuated by severe cyclones requiring vessel sail-away; operability planning is cyclone-track / forecast-driven rather than fixed-Hs-limited

gaps: all numeric extreme values (Hs/Hmax/Tp/wind/current by return period) - only qualitative regime description obtained; primary tables are in paywalled OnePetro papers (e.g. OTC-20732-MS)
non-cyclone operational sea-state scatter

citations.source: Metocean Extreme Hurricane/Cyclone Design Criteria for LNG Developments using long synthetic database (OTC-20732-MS); global tropical-cyclone extreme wave climatology (Nature Sci Reports) - secondary/qualitative

records.name: DNV-RP-C205 - methodology layer for environmental conditions & loads (all regions)

fields.scope: Rational design criteria & guidance for wind, wave, current and tide loads on marine structures incl. ships, fixed/floating offshore units, mobile units, marine renewables
fields.provides: Short-term (per sea-state) statistical models; long-term joint metocean distributions; single-parameter and joint Hs-Tp distributions for design load conditions across limit states
fields.scatter_diagram_role: Defines wave scatter diagram concept (Hs vs Tp/Tz cell occurrence) and joint long-term models used to build region-specific scatter diagrams for fatigue & operability/persistence analysis
fields.wind_profile: Logarithmic/power-law wind profiles, gust factors, NPD/Frya and Harris/Kaimal wind spectra (consistent with the dimensional 1-hr-mean profile formulas reproduced in API 2INT-MET Sec 4.1)
fields.current: Current profile models (tidal + wind-driven + density-driven), depth variation
fields.limit_state_use: ULS/ALS extreme return-period values and FLS scatter diagrams; operability uses short-term/seasonal statistics
fields.relationship_to_regional_records: C205 is the methodology; regional numbers (North Sea, GoM, etc.) are produced by applying C205-type models to local hindcast/measurement data

gaps: actual standard text and tables are paywalled (DNV store) - not transcribed; no region-specific numeric criteria in C205 itself (it is a method, not a regional dataset)

citations.source: DNV-RP-C205 Environmental conditions and environmental loads (official DNV page / GlobalSpec abstract)
API Bulletin 2INT-MET Sec 4.1 (reproduces the same 1-hr-mean wind profile, gust and spectrum formulas used in DNV/ISO practice) - free corroboration of methodology
